steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows a date within a relative clause. The next clause — 'Page's only model had been suspiciously destroyed' — is a complete independent clause. The blank must create a legal sentence boundary between the relative clause and that independent clause.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 1906—roughly six weeks earlier—Page's: the em-dash pair leaves the independent clause beginning with "Page's" attached to the previous clause with only a comma, creating a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — 1906, roughly six weeks earlier, Page's: commas set off "roughly six weeks earlier," but the comma before "Page's" leaves a comma splice between the relative clause and the independent clause. ✗
  • Choice C — roughly six weeks earlier, Page's: the comma after 'earlier' leaves a comma splice between the relative clause and the independent clause that follows. ✗
  • Choice D — 1906—roughly six weeks earlier. Page's: the dash introduces the parenthetical comparison to the Wright brothers' May 22 patent date, and the period creates a full sentence boundary so 'Page's only model had been…' begins a new, complete sentence. ✓
D is correct.
